picks.rationale: Up +237% YTD, +40.22% in the last month, P/E of 34.14 is reasonable, news sentiment is strong at +0.408, and the 1-day gain of +1.96% shows momentum resilience during the recent broad selloff.
Up +645% over 1 year and +125.9% YTD with +14.30% in the last month, strong buy analyst consensus, positive news sentiment at +0.15, while the 1D move of +1.66% shows continued momentum.
1-year return of +594%, YTD +135.05% and a 1-month gain of +7.08% show robust momentum, backed by a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.29) despite negative news sentiment.

YTD return of +82.36% and a 1-month gain of +10.58% with positive news sentiment (+0.226) and strong_buy consensus; short interest is high at 25.59% which can drive sharp rallies on positive catalysts.
YTD surge of +714.98% and a 1-month return of +41.33% despite a high P/E of 91.95; the 1D move of +0.42% avoided the worst of Tuesday's memory selloff, supported by positive news sentiment of 0.164.

Up +852% over 1 year and +233.59% YTD, with a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.49) and a reasonable P/E of 49.75; the -14.52% 1D drop is a sharp pullback but driven by Korea leveraged ETF contagion, not fundamentals, and Dan Ives calls it a 'gut check moment' with Micron earnings the next catalyst ([fool.com](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/06/23/why-micron-stock-suddenly-crashed/), [benzinga.com](https://www.benzinga.com/markets/equities/26/06/60062350/dan-ives-says-ai-revolution-is-in-the-third-inning-calls-chip-sell-off-a-gut-check-moment)).

Up +257.62% YTD and +27.88% in the last month with a buy analyst consensus and neutral news sentiment; the -8.53% 1D drop follows sector-wide Korea-led selling but the 1W gain of +2.63% shows relative strength.

YTD of +613.41% and 1M of +23.53% with a buy analyst consensus; the -13.31% 1D crash on Korean contagion is severe, but the underlying Q3 beat (revenue $5.95B vs $4.73B consensus) and 645% datacenter YoY growth suggest fundamentals remain intact ([247wallst.com](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/06/23/sandisk-plunges-11-micron-and-western-digital-slide-10-as-korean-market-crash-hits-memory-chips/)).

YTD return of +261.83% and a 1-month gain of +22.80% with strong news sentiment (+0.141) and a buy consensus; the -5.04% 1D dip is modest relative to peers, and the 1W gain of +1.94% shows relative resilience.

1-year return of +305% and YTD +212.54% with a strong_buy consensus (score 1.45); the -8.08% 1D drop reflects broad AI spending fears, but the 1M gain of +33.99% underscores strong underlying demand for data infrastructure.

previous_picks.rationale: Momentum is extreme (+63.76% 1M, +297.86% YTD) with an 8% dividend yield, positive news sentiment of 0.213, and a strong analyst score of 1.56; web search confirms [Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/06/21/dont-forget-about-western-digital-amid-sandisk-euphoria/) highlights WDC as a beneficiary of AI storage demand with 45% YoY revenue growth.

Analyst consensus is strong_buy with a top score of 1.48, and the stock has surged +62.29% 1M and +259.67% YTD; Micron's upcoming earnings on June 24 are cited as a key catalyst in web results, with all three major firms raising price targets.

The stock posted the highest YTD return (+857.71%) and 1M gain (+89.37%) among all names, with a buy analyst rating and 0.164 news sentiment; this Japan memory stock is riding the AI semiconductor wave alongside peers.

SNDK has exploded +693.76% YTD and +57.94% 1M on a NAND flash shortage, with CEO commentary at Mizuho about data center becoming its largest market; however, extreme overbought conditions (beta 3.45, high short interest 9.71%) warrant caution.

MRVL shows a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.45), impressive momentum (+76.20% 1M, +247.87% YTD), and positive news sentiment 0.149; it recently hit 52-week highs and is a key beneficiary of AI infrastructure buildout.

STX has strong momentum (+45.94% 1M, +272.85% YTD) with an 28% dividend yield and a buy analyst rating (score 1.54); web search notes retail sentiment bullish and a 55% rally in six sessions for peer WDC, indicating memory sector strength.

DELL surged +74.06% in the last month and +222.82% YTD, with a reasonable P/E of 32.68 and a 62% dividend yield; the stock is cited as 15.4% undervalued as the AI narrative builds, supported by strong data center demand.

CRDO has a strong_buy analyst rating (score 1.33), solid momentum (+60.86% 1M, +89.80% YTD), and is a key player in the DSP/semi segment; positive news sentiment of 0.084 and a beta of 3.229 indicate high growth potential.

WULF has explosive momentum (+35.80% 1M, +127.47% YTD) and a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.33), though short interest is high at 25.85%; the stock benefits from AI compute demand as an AI data center operator.

TLN has a strong_buy analyst rating (score 1.5) with exceptional 1M (+38.69%) and 1W (+26.53%) momentum; news sentiment is 0.227, and the stock surged after signing a uranium deal, aligning with the nuclear power theme for AI.
